content_preview.title: Arm Breaks a 35-Year Rule by Shipping Its First Production Chip, Betting Silicon Sales Will Eclipse Licensing
content_preview.summary: Arm unveiled the AGI CPU on March 24, its first production chip in 35 years, targeting data center AI workloads with Meta as lead customer and OpenAI, Cloudflare, and Cerebras signed on.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

For nearly 36 years, Arm Holdings operated as one of the most consequential companies in the semiconductor industry without ever selling a chip. The Cambridge-based firm licensed its processor designs to Apple, Qualcomm, Samsung, and hundreds of other companies, collecting royalties while keeping its hands clean of the messy, capital-intensive business of actually fabricating silicon. On March 24, 2026, that era ended.

Arm unveiled the AGI CPU — its first production-ready processor...
